title: iguanura asli
intro: Iguanura asli, also known as the "Malaysian giant" or "Malaysian wild ginger", is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Malaysia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, derived from its rhizome. Iguanura asli is traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and skin infections, thanks to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the rhizome of Iguanura asli is typically used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are often consumed to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
